ringbuffer.c(3) Library Functions Manual ringbuffer.c(3)

NAME

ringbuffer.c - 🎯 Lock-free circular buffer for audio streaming with atomic operations

SYNOPSIS

Functions


ringbuffer_t * ringbuffer_create (size_t element_size, size_t capacity)
void ringbuffer_destroy (ringbuffer_t *rb)
bool ringbuffer_write (ringbuffer_t *rb, const void *data)
bool ringbuffer_read (ringbuffer_t *rb, void *data)
bool ringbuffer_peek (ringbuffer_t *rb, void *data)
size_t ringbuffer_size (const ringbuffer_t *rb)
bool ringbuffer_is_empty (const ringbuffer_t *rb)
bool ringbuffer_is_full (const ringbuffer_t *rb)
void ringbuffer_clear (ringbuffer_t *rb)
framebuffer_t * framebuffer_create (size_t capacity)
framebuffer_t * framebuffer_create_multi (size_t capacity)
void framebuffer_destroy (framebuffer_t *fb)
bool framebuffer_write_frame (framebuffer_t *fb, const char *frame_data, size_t frame_size)
bool framebuffer_read_frame (framebuffer_t *fb, frame_t *frame)
void framebuffer_clear (framebuffer_t *fb)
bool framebuffer_write_multi_frame (framebuffer_t *fb, const char *frame_data, size_t frame_size, uint32_t source_client_id, uint32_t frame_sequence, uint32_t timestamp)
bool framebuffer_read_multi_frame (framebuffer_t *fb, multi_source_frame_t *frame)
bool framebuffer_peek_latest_multi_frame (framebuffer_t *fb, multi_source_frame_t *frame)

Detailed Description

🎯 Lock-free circular buffer for audio streaming with atomic operations

Definition in file ringbuffer.c.

Function Documentation

void framebuffer_clear (framebuffer_t * fb)

Definition at line 322 of file ringbuffer.c.

322                                           {
323   if (!fb || !fb->rb)
324     return;
325 
326   // Thread-safe access to framebuffer (was missing, causing race conditions)
327   mutex_lock(&fb->mutex);
328 
329   // Check the element size to determine frame type
330   if (fb->rb->element_size == sizeof(multi_source_frame_t)) {
331     // Multi-source frame buffer - read and free multi_source_frame_t
332     multi_source_frame_t multi_frame;
333     while (ringbuffer_read(fb->rb, &multi_frame)) {
334       if (IS_FRAME_VALID((frame_t *)&multi_frame)) {
335         MARK_FRAME_FREED((frame_t *)&multi_frame); // Mark as freed to detect use-after-free
336         buffer_pool_free(NULL, multi_frame.data, multi_frame.size);
337       } else if (!IS_MAGIC_VALID(multi_frame.magic, FRAME_MAGIC) && multi_frame.magic != 0) {
338         SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_STATE, "CORRUPTION: Invalid multi-source frame magic 0x%x during clear",
339                   multi_frame.magic);
340       }
341     }
342   } else if (fb->rb->element_size == sizeof(frame_t)) {
343     // Single-source frame buffer - read and free frame_t
344     frame_t frame;
345     while (ringbuffer_read(fb->rb, &frame)) {
346       if (IS_FRAME_VALID(&frame)) {
347         MARK_FRAME_FREED(&frame); // Mark as freed to detect use-after-free
348         buffer_pool_free(NULL, frame.data, frame.size);
349       } else if (!IS_MAGIC_VALID(frame.magic, FRAME_MAGIC) && frame.magic != 0) {
350         SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_STATE, "CORRUPTION: Invalid frame magic 0x%x during clear", frame.magic);
351       }
352     }
353   } else {
354     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_STATE, "Unknown frame buffer type with element size %zu", fb->rb->element_size);
355   }
356 
357   // Clear the ringbuffer indices
358   ringbuffer_clear(fb->rb);
359 
360   // Zero out the entire buffer to prevent any dangling pointers
361   if (fb->rb->buffer) {
362     // Check for integer overflow before multiplication
363     if (fb->rb->capacity > SIZE_MAX / fb->rb->element_size) {
364       SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "Buffer size would overflow: capacity=%zu, element_size=%zu", fb->rb->capacity,
365                 fb->rb->element_size);
366       mutex_unlock(&fb->mutex);
367       return;
368     }
369     size_t buffer_size = fb->rb->capacity * fb->rb->element_size;
370     SAFE_MEMSET(fb->rb->buffer, buffer_size, 0, buffer_size);
371   }
372 
373   mutex_unlock(&fb->mutex);
374 }




References buffer_pool_free(), buffer_size,
    frame, ringbuffer_clear(), and ringbuffer_read().


Referenced by framebuffer_destroy().






framebuffer_t
  * framebuffer_create (size_t capacity)


Definition at line 146 of file ringbuffer.c.


146                                                    {
147   if (capacity == 0) {
148     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "Invalid frame buffer parameters");
149     return NULL;
150   }
151 
152   framebuffer_t *fb;
153   fb = SAFE_CALLOC(1, sizeof(framebuffer_t), framebuffer_t *);
154 
155   // Initialize mutex for thread-safe access
156   if (mutex_init(&fb->mutex, "framebuffer") != 0) {
157     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_THREAD, "Failed to initialize framebuffer mutex");
158     SAFE_FREE(fb);
159     return NULL;
160   }
161 
162   // Create ringbuffer to store frame_t structs
163   fb->rb = ringbuffer_create(sizeof(frame_t), capacity);
164   if (!fb->rb) {
165     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_MEMORY, "Failed to allocate frame buffer");
166     mutex_destroy(&fb->mutex);
167     SAFE_FREE(fb);
168     return NULL;
169   }
170 
171   return fb;
172 }






References mutex_destroy(), mutex_init(), and
    ringbuffer_create().






framebuffer_t
  * framebuffer_create_multi (size_t capacity)


Definition at line 174 of file ringbuffer.c.


174                                                          {
175   if (capacity == 0) {
176     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "Invalid capacity: %zu", capacity);
177     return NULL;
178   }
179 
180   framebuffer_t *fb;
181   fb = SAFE_CALLOC(1, sizeof(framebuffer_t), framebuffer_t *);
182 
183   // Initialize mutex for thread-safe access
184   if (mutex_init(&fb->mutex, "framebuffer_multi") != 0) {
185     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_THREAD, "Failed to initialize framebuffer mutex");
186     SAFE_FREE(fb);
187     return NULL;
188   }
189 
190   // Create ringbuffer to store multi_source_frame_t structs
191   fb->rb = ringbuffer_create(sizeof(multi_source_frame_t), capacity);
192 
193   if (!fb->rb) {
194     mutex_destroy(&fb->mutex);
195     SAFE_FREE(fb);
196     return NULL;
197   }
198 
199   return fb;
200 }

void
  framebuffer_destroy (framebuffer_t * fb)


Definition at line 202 of file ringbuffer.c.


202                                             {
203   if (!fb)
204     return;
205 
206   // Add magic number check to detect double-free using rb pointer
207   if (fb->rb == (ringbuffer_t *)0xDEADBEEF) {
208     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_STATE, "DOUBLE-FREE DETECTED: framebuffer %p already destroyed!", fb);
209     return;
210   }
211 
212   framebuffer_clear(fb);
213   ringbuffer_destroy(fb->rb);
214   mutex_destroy(&fb->mutex);
215 
216   // Mark as destroyed before freeing
217   fb->rb = (ringbuffer_t *)0xDEADBEEF;
218   SAFE_FREE(fb);
219 }






References framebuffer_clear(), mutex_destroy(), and
    ringbuffer_destroy().






bool framebuffer_peek_latest_multi_frame (framebuffer_t *
  fb, multi_source_frame_t * frame)


Definition at line 459 of file ringbuffer.c.


459                                                                                          {
460   if (!fb || !fb->rb || !frame) {
461     return false;
462   }
463 
464   // Thread-safe access to framebuffer
465   mutex_lock(&fb->mutex);
466 
467   // Use ringbuffer_peek to get the frame without consuming it
468   bool result = ringbuffer_peek(fb->rb, frame);
469 
470   if (result) {
471     // Validate frame magic
472     if (!IS_MAGIC_VALID(frame->magic, FRAME_MAGIC)) {
473       SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_STATE, "CORRUPTION: Invalid multi-source frame magic 0x%x (expected 0x%x) in peek",
474                 frame->magic, FRAME_MAGIC);
475       frame->data = NULL;
476       frame->size = 0;
477       memset(frame->source_client_id, 0, sizeof(frame->source_client_id));
478       mutex_unlock(&fb->mutex);
479       return false;
480     }
481 
482     // Additional validation
483     if (frame->size == 0 || !frame->data) {
484       SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_STATE, "CORRUPTION: Invalid multi-source frame data (size=%zu, data=%p) in peek",
485                 frame->size, frame->data);
486       mutex_unlock(&fb->mutex);
487       return false;
488     }
489 
490     // IMPORTANT: We need to make a copy of the data since we're not consuming the frame
491     // The original data pointer will remain valid in the ring buffer
492     // Caller is responsible for freeing this copy with buffer_pool_free()
493     char *data_copy;
494     data_copy = (char *)buffer_pool_alloc(NULL, frame->size);
495     if (!data_copy) {
496       SET_ERRNO(ERROR_MEMORY, "Failed to allocate memory for frame data copy in peek");
497       mutex_unlock(&fb->mutex);
498       return false;
499     }
500     SAFE_MEMCPY(data_copy, frame->size, frame->data, frame->size);
501     frame->data = data_copy;
502   }
503 
504   mutex_unlock(&fb->mutex);
505   return result;
506 }






References buffer_pool_alloc(), frame, and
    ringbuffer_peek().






bool framebuffer_read_frame (framebuffer_t * fb, frame_t *
  frame)


Definition at line 275 of file ringbuffer.c.


275                                                                {
276   if (!fb || !frame) {
277     return false;
278   }
279 
280   // Initialize frame to safe values
281   frame->magic = 0;
282   frame->data = NULL;
283   frame->size = 0;
284 
285   // Thread-safe access to framebuffer (fixes TOCTOU race condition)
286   mutex_lock(&fb->mutex);
287 
288   bool result = ringbuffer_read(fb->rb, frame);
289 
290   // Validate the frame we just read
291   if (result) {
292     if (!IS_MAGIC_VALID(frame->magic, FRAME_MAGIC)) {
293       SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_STATE, "CORRUPTION: Invalid frame magic 0x%x (expected 0x%x)", frame->magic, FRAME_MAGIC);
294       frame->data = NULL;
295       frame->size = 0;
296       mutex_unlock(&fb->mutex);
297       return false;
298     }
299 
300     if (IS_FRAME_FREED(frame)) {
301       SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_STATE, "CORRUPTION: Reading already-freed frame!");
302       frame->data = NULL;
303       frame->size = 0;
304       mutex_unlock(&fb->mutex);
305       return false;
306     }
307 
308     if (frame->size > 10 * 1024 * 1024) {
309       SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_STATE, "CORRUPTION: Frame size too large: %zu", frame->size);
310       SAFE_FREE(frame->data);
311       frame->data = NULL;
312       frame->size = 0;
313       mutex_unlock(&fb->mutex);
314       return false;
315     }
316   }
317 
318   mutex_unlock(&fb->mutex);
319   return result;
320 }






References frame, and ringbuffer_read().






bool framebuffer_read_multi_frame (framebuffer_t * fb,

425   if (!fb || !fb->rb || !frame) {

427   }
428

430   mutex_lock(&fb->mutex);
431 
432   bool result = ringbuffer_read(fb->rb, frame);
433 
434   if (result) {
435     // Validate frame magic
436     if (!IS_MAGIC_VALID(frame->magic, FRAME_MAGIC)) {
437       SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_STATE, "CORRUPTION: Invalid multi-source frame magic 0x%x (expected 0x%x)", frame->magic,
438                 FRAME_MAGIC);
439       frame->data = NULL;
440       frame->size = 0;
441       memset(frame->source_client_id, 0, sizeof(frame->source_client_id));
442       mutex_unlock(&fb->mutex);
443       return false;
444     }
445 
446     // Additional validation

448       SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_STATE, "CORRUPTION: Invalid multi-source frame data (size=%zu, data=%p)", frame->size,

450       mutex_unlock(&fb->mutex);
451       return false;
452     }
453   }
454 
455   mutex_unlock(&fb->mutex);
456   return result;

bool framebuffer_write_frame (framebuffer_t * fb, const
  char * frame_data, size_t frame_size)


Definition at line 221 of file ringbuffer.c.


221                                                                                            {
222   if (!fb || !frame_data || frame_size == 0)
223     return false;
224 
225   // Validate frame size to prevent overflow
226   if (frame_size > 10 * 1024 * 1024) { // 10MB max for ASCII frames
227     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "Rejecting oversized frame: %zu bytes", frame_size);
228     return false;
229   }
230 
231   // Thread-safe access to framebuffer (was missing, causing race conditions)
232   mutex_lock(&fb->mutex);
233 
234   // Check if buffer is full - if so, we need to drop the oldest frame
235   if (ringbuffer_size(fb->rb) >= fb->rb->capacity) {
236     // Buffer is full, read and free the oldest frame before writing new one
237     frame_t old_frame;
238     if (ringbuffer_read(fb->rb, &old_frame)) {
239       if (IS_FRAME_VALID(&old_frame)) {
240         MARK_FRAME_FREED(&old_frame);
241         // Use buffer_pool_free since data was allocated with buffer_pool_alloc
242         buffer_pool_free(NULL, old_frame.data, old_frame.size);
243       } else if (!IS_MAGIC_VALID(old_frame.magic, FRAME_MAGIC)) {
244         SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_STATE, "CORRUPTION: Invalid old frame magic 0x%x when dropping", old_frame.magic);
245       }
246     }
247   }
248 
249   // Allocate a copy of the frame data using buffer pool for better performance
250   char *frame_copy = (char *)buffer_pool_alloc(NULL, frame_size + 1);
251   if (!frame_copy) {
252     mutex_unlock(&fb->mutex);
253     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_MEMORY, "Failed to allocate %zu bytes from buffer pool for frame", frame_size + 1);
254     return false;
255   }
256 
257   SAFE_MEMCPY(frame_copy, frame_size, frame_data, frame_size);
258   frame_copy[frame_size] = '\0'; // Ensure null termination
259 
260   // Create a frame_t struct with the copy (store allocated size for proper cleanup)
261   frame_t frame = {.magic = MAGIC_FRAME_VALID, .size = frame_size + 1, .data = frame_copy};
262 
263   bool result = ringbuffer_write(fb->rb, &frame);
264 
265   if (!result) {
266     // If we still couldn't write to ringbuffer, return the buffer to pool
267     buffer_pool_free(NULL, frame_copy, frame_size + 1);
268     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_STATE, "Failed to write frame to ringbuffer even after dropping oldest");
269   }
270 
271   mutex_unlock(&fb->mutex);
272   return result;
273 }






References buffer_pool_alloc(), buffer_pool_free(),
    frame, ringbuffer_read(), ringbuffer_size(), and
    ringbuffer_write().






bool framebuffer_write_multi_frame (framebuffer_t * fb,
  const char * frame_data, size_t frame_size, uint32_t source_client_id,
  uint32_t frame_sequence, uint32_t timestamp)


Definition at line 378 of file ringbuffer.c.


379                                                                                                            {
380   if (!fb || !fb->rb || !frame_data || frame_size == 0) {
381     return false;
382   }
383 
384   // Allocate memory for frame data using buffer pool for better performance
385   char *data_copy = (char *)buffer_pool_alloc(NULL, frame_size);
386   if (!data_copy) {
387     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_MEMORY, "Failed to allocate %zu bytes from buffer pool for multi-source frame", frame_size);
388     return false;
389   }
390 
391   // Copy frame data
392   SAFE_MEMCPY(data_copy, frame_size, frame_data, frame_size);
393 
394   // Convert client ID number to string for storage in frame metadata
395   char client_id_str[64];
396   snprintf(client_id_str, sizeof(client_id_str), "%u", source_client_id);
397 
398   // Create multi-source frame
399   multi_source_frame_t multi_frame = {
400       .magic = MAGIC_FRAME_VALID,
401       .source_client_id = {0},
402       .frame_sequence = frame_sequence,
403       .timestamp = timestamp,
404       .size = frame_size,
405       .data = data_copy,
406   };
407   SAFE_MEMCPY(multi_frame.source_client_id, sizeof(multi_frame.source_client_id), client_id_str, strlen(client_id_str) + 1);
408 
409   // Thread-safe access to framebuffer
410   mutex_lock(&fb->mutex);
411 
412   // Try to write to ring buffer
413   bool success = ringbuffer_write(fb->rb, &multi_frame);
414   if (!success) {
415     // Buffer full, return the buffer to pool
416     buffer_pool_free(NULL, data_copy, frame_size);
417     log_debug("Frame buffer full, dropping multi-source frame from client %u", source_client_id);
418   }
419 
420   mutex_unlock(&fb->mutex);
421   return success;
422 }






References buffer_pool_alloc(), buffer_pool_free(),
    and ringbuffer_write().






void
  ringbuffer_clear (ringbuffer_t * rb)


Definition at line 133 of file ringbuffer.c.


133                                         {
134   if (rb) {
135     atomic_store_u64(&rb->head, 0);
136     atomic_store_u64(&rb->tail, 0);

ringbuffer_t
  * ringbuffer_create (size_t element_size, size_t capacity)


Definition at line 29 of file ringbuffer.c.


29                                                                       {
30   if (element_size == 0 || capacity == 0) {
31     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "Invalid ring buffer parameters: element_size=%zu, capacity=%zu", element_size,
32               capacity);
33     return NULL;
34   }
35 
36   ringbuffer_t *rb;
37   rb = SAFE_CALLOC(1, sizeof(ringbuffer_t), ringbuffer_t *);
38 
39   /* Round capacity up to power of 2 for optimization */
40   size_t actual_capacity = math_next_power_of_two(capacity);
41 
42   rb->buffer = SAFE_CALLOC(actual_capacity, element_size, char *);
43 
44   rb->element_size = element_size;
45   rb->capacity = actual_capacity;
46   rb->is_power_of_two = true;
47   rb->capacity_mask = actual_capacity - 1;
48   /* Atomic fields are zero-initialized by SAFE_CALLOC */
49 
50   return rb;
51 }

bool ringbuffer_peek (ringbuffer_t * rb, void * data)


Definition at line 104 of file ringbuffer.c.


104                                                    {
105   if (!rb || !data)
106     return false;
107 
108   size_t current_size = atomic_load_u64(&rb->size);
109   if (current_size == 0) {
110     return false; /* Buffer empty */
111   }
112 
113   size_t tail = atomic_load_u64(&rb->tail);
114 
115   /* Copy data without updating tail */
116   SAFE_MEMCPY(data, rb->element_size, rb->buffer + (tail * rb->element_size), rb->element_size);
117 
118   return true;
119 }






References atomic_load_u64().


Referenced by framebuffer_peek_latest_multi_frame().






bool ringbuffer_read (ringbuffer_t * rb, void * data)


Definition at line 82 of file ringbuffer.c.


82                                                    {
83   if (!rb || !data)
84     return false;
85 
86   size_t current_size = atomic_load_u64(&rb->size);
87   if (current_size == 0) {
88     return false; /* Buffer empty */
89   }
90 
91   size_t tail = atomic_load_u64(&rb->tail);
92   size_t next_tail = (tail + 1) & rb->capacity_mask;
93 
94   /* Copy data */
95   SAFE_MEMCPY(data, rb->element_size, rb->buffer + (tail * rb->element_size), rb->element_size);
96 
97   /* Update tail and size atomically */
98   atomic_store_u64(&rb->tail, next_tail);
99   atomic_fetch_sub_u64(&rb->size, 1);
100 
101   return true;
102 }






References atomic_fetch_sub_u64(),
    atomic_load_u64(), and atomic_store_u64().

bool ringbuffer_write (ringbuffer_t * rb, const void *
  data)


Definition at line 60 of file ringbuffer.c.


60                                                           {
61   if (!rb || !data)
62     return false;
63 
64   size_t current_size = atomic_load_u64(&rb->size);
65   if (current_size >= rb->capacity) {
66     return false; /* Buffer full */
67   }
68 
69   size_t head = atomic_load_u64(&rb->head);
70   size_t next_head = (head + 1) & rb->capacity_mask;
71 
72   /* Copy data */
73   SAFE_MEMCPY(rb->buffer + (head * rb->element_size), rb->element_size, data, rb->element_size);
74 
75   /* Update head and size atomically */
76   atomic_store_u64(&rb->head, next_head);
77   atomic_fetch_add_u64(&rb->size, 1);
78 
79   return true;
80 }






References atomic_fetch_add_u64(),
    atomic_load_u64(), and atomic_store_u64().
